codeReview.summary: The reviewed change does not implement the requested fix. Commit 1269490 only updates the Playwright test to click Calendar after returning to /timer, which works around persisted TimerView state instead of restoring the documented default-calendar behavior for the workspace-switch return flow.

issues.description: After navigating back to /timer in workspace B, the test now clicks the Calendar tab before asserting VAL-REG-002. The feature requires the page itself to land with Calendar active again, so this change masks the regression rather than fixing it.

The same manual Calendar re-selection is repeated when switching back to workspace A. Because the assertions only pass after an extra click, the commit still does not prove or enforce that returning to /timer reactivates Calendar automatically.

The timer page state still initializes from loadPersistedTimerView() and persists alternate views. The reviewed commit does not change this return-path behavior, so the runtime can still reopen /timer in list or timesheet instead of Calendar after a workspace switch.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker handoff itself says the regression was handled by changing the test, not by a scoped code fix, and no manual/browser confirmation was recorded for a behavior-changing fix review.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff 2026-03-25T03-32-24-006Z__tracking-timer-calendar-return-regression-fix__ee376bf3-e088-4eeb-ad67-7e373ff1ba52.json says in whatWasLeftUndone that the regression is addressed by the test's explicit Calendar click, not by code change; worker-transcripts.jsonl for session ee376bf3-e088-4eeb-ad67-7e373ff1ba52 shows interactiveChecks = []. The frontend-test-worker skill requires code-fix handoffs to point at the commit containing the behavior change and to record browser confirmation or an explicit conflict justification.

summary: Fail: commit 1269490 only updates VAL-REG-002 to click Calendar after returning to /timer, while the timer page still restores persisted view state from local storage. The requested behavior—that the workspace-switch return flow lands on /timer with Calendar active again—remains unimplemented.
